learningObjectives.title: Understand planning constraints
learningObjectives.description: Explain mandatory rules that must not be violated during transportation planning.

learningObjectives.title: Understand planning profiles
learningObjectives.description: Describe profile components controlling cockpit and optimizer behavior.

learningObjectives.title: Differentiate profile and constraint
learningObjectives.description: Clarify planning behavior configuration versus planning limitations.

sections.content: Transportation Planning in SAP TM is governed by Planning Constraints and Planning Profiles. Constraints define the rules that must never be violated during planning, while Planning Profiles determine how the optimizer and Transportation Cockpit execute transportation planning.

sections.title: Key Concept
sections.content: Planning Profiles define how planning is executed, whereas Planning Constraints define what the optimizer is allowed or not allowed to do.

A refrigerated food company configures a Planning Profile that prioritizes delivery time over transportation cost. The optimizer only selects refrigerated vehicles, validates driver schedules and ensures all deliveries reach customers within the committed delivery window.

sections.title: Best Practice
sections.content: Keep Planning Profiles standardized across similar business processes. Avoid creating excessive profile variations unless business requirements clearly justify them.

sections.title: Real Project Example
sections.content: A pharmaceutical company created separate Planning Profiles for domestic and export shipments. Domestic planning prioritized transportation cost, while export planning prioritized transit time and regulatory compliance.

sections.title: Interview Tip
sections.content: Certification questions frequently distinguish Planning Profiles from Planning Constraints. Profiles define planning behavior; constraints define planning limitations.

sections.title: Common Mistake
sections.content: Do not configure optimization objectives that conflict with mandatory planning constraints. Constraints always take precedence over optimization goals.

items: Planning Profiles configure transportation planning behavior.
Planning Constraints define mandatory business rules.
The optimizer validates all constraints before creating Freight Orders.
Profiles improve consistency across planning activities.
Constraints ensure legal and operational compliance.
